title: Persistent Data Reliability Test Blueprint
content: This blueprint defines a practical persistence test approach for backend services that must guarantee durability, consistency, and recoverability. Purpose: confirm that data acknowledged by the API is safely stored, remains correct under concurrent access, and can be restored after failures. Start with schema and constraint validation by testing primary keys, foreign keys, unique indexes, required fields, and valid enum/domain values. Execute CRUD durability tests with normal, boundary, and malformed payloads to verify accepted writes persist and rejected writes never alter state. Validate transactional integrity by forcing failures between multi-step operations and ensuring full rollback with zero partial commits. Add idempotency checks using repeated requests with identical idempotency keys to prove exactly-once outcomes for retry scenarios. Run concurrency tests with parallel updates to the same records to detect lost updates, stale writes, and lock contention behavior; verify version checks or locking policies work as intended. Perform restart resilience tests by writing data, restarting application and database components, then confirming committed data remains intact and query performance stays within baseline. Inject fault scenarios such as network interruption, storage latency spikes, deadlocks, and abrupt process termination during write operations to evaluate recovery and reconciliation logic. Cover migration safety by applying forward schema migrations on realistic datasets, validating data compatibility, and rehearsing rollback plans without truncation or semantic drift. Verify backup and restore readiness with scheduled snapshots, checksum and row-count comparisons after restore, and application-level read/write smoke tests in an isolated environment. Include observability requirements: structured audit logs for all mutations, correlation IDs across services, persistence error-rate dashboards, and actionable alert thresholds. Define acceptance criteria: no critical data integrity defects, successful disaster recovery drill within target RTO/RPO, and documented remediation owners with deadlines for any non-critical findings.
